title: Unauthorized VPN Tunnel into OT Network
spl:
index=ot_security sourcetype="ot:security:vpn" OR (index=ot sourcetype="ics:firewall" dest_port IN (443, 1194, 4500))
| lookup ot_approved_vpn_peers remote_ip user OUTPUT approved_site
| where isnull(approved_site)
| stats earliest(_time) as start latest(_time) as end by remote_ip, user, assigned_ip, gateway
description: Detects VPN or encrypted sessions into OT that violate perimeter policy (T0886), supporting regulatory and segmentation requirements.
value: Detects VPN or encrypted sessions into OT that violate perimeter policy (T0886), supporting regulatory and segmentation requirements.
implementation: Maintain approved vendor and employee VPN profiles. Alert on split tunnel to OT, new device IDs. Map to T0886.
visualization: Timeline of VPN sessions, Table of unauthorized peers.
knownFalsePositives: OT firewall denies from authorized engineering access, vendor remote support, or scheduled maintenance VPN sessions can look like tunnel abuse — compare to the approved slot and source identity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: OT Security Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 5151).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ot_security` VPN concentrator logs, firewall sessions.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Maintain approved vendor and employee VPN profiles. Alert on split tunnel to OT, new device IDs. Map to T0886.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot_security sourcetype="ot:security:vpn" OR (index=ot sourcetype="ics:firewall" dest_port IN (443, 1194, 4500))
| lookup ot_approved_vpn_peers remote_ip user OUTPUT approved_site
| where isnull(approved_site)
| stats earliest(_time) as start latest(_time) as end by remote_ip, user, assigned_ip, gateway
```

Understanding this SPL

**Unauthorized VPN Tunnel into OT Network** — Detects VPN or encrypted sessions into OT that violate perimeter policy (T0886), supporting regulatory and segmentation requirements.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ot_security` VPN concentrator logs, firewall sessions. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): OT Security Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 5151). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_security, ot; **sourcetype**: ot:security:vpn, ics:firewall. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot_security, index=ot, sourcetype="ot:security:vpn". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(approved_site)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by remote_ip, user, assigned_ip, gateway** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare failed or disabled searches in Splunk to the **OT Security Add-on** configuration UI and the **Saved Searches** list so names and schedules line up. Spot-check one failure against `scheduler.log` around the run time.



Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline of VPN sessions, Table of unauthorized peers.
grandmaExplanation: We look for hidden VPNs or tunnels into the plant that bypass the path we want every remote session to use.
